degraded.detail: classify_complexity estimated 'medium' (prior_source=history, median of last 20 runs) and flagged risk 'touches_billing' — a prose false-positive: the message said 'shallow checkouts' and 'checkout' is a touches_billing keyword (risk_taxonomy.py). The diff touches zero billing paths (one integration test file), so the authoritative risk set is []. Classified as 'small' change per diff-authoritative rule; no mandatory review required.

surface_verification.justification: Test-only change: two pytest.skip hatches in one integration test hardened into hard fetch-depth failures, plus two meta-tests. No runtime code, API route, store mutation, SSE/WS handler, message contract, or UI-consumed code touched — no web or CLI surface to exercise.

behaviors.behavior: the absent-run-id reachability check raises a hard AssertionError naming 'fetch-depth: 0' (not pytest.skip) when a commit is unreachable
the pre-S6 provenance check raises a hard AssertionError naming 'fetch-depth: 0' (not pytest.skip) when PRE_S6_COMMIT is unreachable
on a full-history checkout both checks still pass unchanged (referenced commits reachable → no skip, no raise)

test_completeness.note: Pure-tooling test-hardening iterate; no FR behavior delta, so the cross-layer execution-evidence gate does not fire.
